info.title: IduScore Partner & Platform API
info.description:
Partner-facing JSON API for de-identified clinical submissions (canonical Type A, not FHIR at ingestion).

**Authentication:** Send `X-Api-Key: <key>` **or** `Authorization: Bearer <key>` on every `/v1/*` request (except preflight `OPTIONS`).

**Writes (`POST`):** Client MUST send non-empty `x-correlation-id` and `Idempotency-Key`. The server does not synthesize these from the request id.

**Tenant scope:** Credentials resolve to a tenant; all IDs are tenant-scoped (cross-tenant access returns `404` where applicable).

**Request id:** Responses may echo `x-request-id` when the client sent it; otherwise the server generates one (see error envelope `requestId`).

**Partner responses:** Read APIs return **IduScore wellbeing** language only — stable `wellbeing.*` outcome ids,
plain-English labels, and optional `notices[].message` lines (no internal warning codes or model identifiers).
Use **`GET /openapi.yaml`** for request/response examples aligned with this contract.


---
**Merged spec (this file):** includes **Platform** and **Diagnostics** paths from the service implementation in addition to the **Type A** `/v1` product routes. The slimmer `type-a-partner-v1.*` files are the same contract for submissions, jobs, and results.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description:
Outbound HTTPS callbacks the platform may POST to a URL you register (not paths on the IduScore Partner API;
`/_reference/...` entries document payload and headers for your implementer only).

tags.name: Platform
tags.description: Liveness, readiness, and OpenAPI document discovery. **No API key** required.

tags.name: Diagnostics
tags.description: Validates the same **partner API key** and (for `POST`) write headers as other `/v1` routes. Returns `204` when valid.

get.description: Process is up. Does not verify database connectivity.
Returns `200` when the app can query PostgreSQL; `503` when the database is unavailable.
Serves the canonical `type-a-partner-v1.yaml` (partner HTTP contract) when running the service.

get.summary: OpenAPI specification (JSON) — from disk when build has emitted it
get.description: On a **running** server, returns the prebuilt `openapi.json` (merged) when present; otherwise the server derives JSON from the Type A YAML only.
No body. Returns `204` when `X-Api-Key` or `Authorization: Bearer` is valid for a tenant.

post.summary: Validate partner API key and write headers
post.description: Same as `GET` but requires non-empty `x-correlation-id` and `Idempotency-Key` (same as other mutating `/v1` calls).

post.summary: Submit Type A canonical JSON (async pipeline)
post.description:
Accepts de-identified payload. `partnerId` MUST equal the authenticated `PartnerCredential.id`.

At least one of `markers` or `reportDocuments` MUST be present (non-empty). Extra keys on nested objects are rejected (`.strict()` Zod schemas).

reportDocuments.summaryText: De-identified summary bullets only. No patient name, MRN, or contact details.

202.description: Accepted — submission persisted; pipeline job enqueued

409.description: Idempotency-Key reused with a different request body

error.message: Idempotency-Key was already used with a different request body

get.summary: Get submission detail and job summaries

200.description: Submission scoped to tenant; enforces optional `metadata.partnerId` scoping
Job scoped to tenant; partner scoping follows linked submission metadata when present

get.summary: Get result detail (wellbeing outcome summaries and artifacts)

200.description: Result scoped to tenant; partner scoping follows submission metadata

completedWithWellbeingOutcome.summary: Processing submission with completed wellbeing outcome and PDF artifact
completedWithWellbeingOutcome.description: Outcome uses public `wellbeing.*` ids; notices are partner-safe summaries only.

notices.message: Some optional inputs were not provided.

post.summary: result.ready (outbound to your registered HTTPS URL)
post.description:
**This is not a route on IduScore.** It documents the `POST` the platform sends to **your** configured webhook
callback URL when a result becomes ready. The path on your server is entirely under your control.

**Request body:** `PartnerWebhookResultReadyBody` (JSON, UTF-8; exact bytes are included in the signature).

**Headers:** IduScore sends `Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8` plus the `X-IduScore-*` headers below.
Verify `X-IduScore-Signature` with your signing secret: HMAC-SHA256 over
`X-IduScore-Timestamp + "." +` the raw request body (same string as on the wire). The header value is
`v1=` followed by the hex digest. Optional per-endpoint static headers (not starting with `X-IduScore-`) may be merged in.

**Response:** return any **2xx** to acknowledge; other statuses and transport failures may be retried per platform policy.

parameters.name: X-IduScore-Event
parameters.description: Always `result.ready` for this flow (duplicates `eventType` in the body for routing layers).

parameters.name: X-IduScore-Delivery-Id
parameters.description: Idempotency / support id for this delivery attempt.

parameters.name: X-IduScore-Timestamp
parameters.description: Unix time in seconds (integer as decimal string) used in the HMAC input.

200.description: Webhook received (any 2xx is treated as success)

400.description: Rejected or malformed handling on the receiver (non-2xx may be retried or logged per platform policy)

500.description: Transient or server-side failure on the receiver (non-2xx may be retried per platform policy)

default.description: Other non-success status codes; delivery may be retried or logged according to platform policy.

partnerApiKey.name: X-Api-Key
partnerApiKey.description: Plaintext API key issued for the tenant (stored hashed server-side).

partnerBearer.description: Same secret as X-Api-Key, sent as a Bearer token.

HeaderCorrelationIdRequired.name: x-correlation-id
HeaderCorrelationIdRequired.description: Client-supplied correlation id for writes; bound to error logs and payloads where applicable.

HeaderIdempotencyKeyRequired.name: Idempotency-Key
HeaderIdempotencyKeyRequired.description: Client key for at-most-once submit semantics (24h scope). Reuse with the same body returns the same `submissionId`/`jobId`; reuse with a different body returns 409.

HeaderRequestIdOptional.name: x-request-id
HeaderRequestIdOptional.description: Optional; echoed when possible. Server generates when absent.

BadRequest.description: Validation or missing required headers / bad UUID parameter

Forbidden.description: Tenant inactive, `partnerId` mismatch, or submission restricted to another credential via `metadata.partnerId`

NotFound.description: Resource not found or not visible in tenant scope

correlationId.description: Present when the request established a correlation id (writes always send `x-correlation-id`).

details.description: Optional structured validation or domain hints (shape varies).

TypeASubjectDemographics.description: At least one of `ageYears` or `birthYear` is required at validation time (Zod `superRefine`).

TypeASmoking.description: Cross-field rules (never vs current vs former) enforced in Zod — see `canonical-type-a-v1.contract.ts`.

markerRanges.description: Optional lab-specific **reference** strings keyed by partner marker **display** name (e.g. HbA1c) and/or a marker `code` when the partner prefers. For display in partner reports; not re-validated in-pipeline.

requestedOutcomes.description:
Optional IduScore outcome requests. **Preferred:** public outcome ids — the same values as
**GET /v1/results** → `outcome.outcomeId` (`PartnerOutcomePublicId`), e.g. `wellbeing.cardiovascular`,
`wellbeing.renal`, `wellbeing.cognitive`, `wellbeing.respiratory` (`wellbeing.kidney` and `renal.wellbeing`
are accepted as synonyms for the renal/kidney registry outcome). Legacy string aliases (e.g.
`cardiovascular_wellbeing_10y`) and internal registry stable ids are still accepted and normalized.
Omitted → server default.

clinicalExtensions.description: Optional partner extensions; keys scanned for disallowed direct identifiers. Rules enforced in Zod.

TypeASubmissionBody.description:
Canonical Type A ingestion JSON (`canonical_submission_v1`). At least one of `markers` or `reportDocuments`
must be non-empty (Zod `superRefine`). Cross-field smoking and family-history rules are Zod-only.

status.description:
Partner-facing ingestion outcome for this endpoint. The persisted `Submission.status`
in PostgreSQL continues to reflect pipeline state (e.g. PROCESSING).

SubmissionStatus.description:
Internal PostgreSQL enum (ingestion / workers). Partner GET responses use
`PartnerSubmissionPublicStatus` + `statusLabel` instead of echoing these strings.

PartnerSubmissionPublicStatus.description: Stable public slug for submission pipeline state (partner JSON).

PartnerOutcomePublicId.description: Stable public IduScore outcome id (not internal persistence stem ids).

PartnerProcessingJobTypePublic.description: Stable public id for a pipeline step (not `ProcessingJobType`).

PartnerProcessingJobStatusPublic.description: Stable public id for job lifecycle (not `ProcessingJobStatus`).

slaDeadlineAt.description: Deadline derived from acceptedAt + platform SLA at submission time for ingest jobs

processingDurationMs.description: Milliseconds from startedAt (else acceptedAt) to terminal time when finished

slaBreached.description: True if in-flight past deadline or terminal end after slaDeadlineAt

PartnerInferenceSnapshotV1.description:
Legacy richer partner projection (not returned by current GET `/v1/results/:id`; see `PartnerOutcomeSummaryPublicV1`).
Retained in the spec for historical reference.

wellbeingFocus.description: Public label such as Cardiovascular Wellbeing (never a raw stable outcome id).

PartnerOutcomeSummaryPublicV1.description:
Minimal public outcome for GET `/v1/results/:id` — display labels and a single scalar score only.
No model/provider/feature vocabulary, thresholds, probabilities as separate fields, or machine warning codes.

focus.description: Public wellbeing focus title (e.g. Cardiovascular Wellbeing).

PartnerResultArtifactItem.description:
Partner-safe artifact reference for GET result. Excludes storage buckets, object keys, gs:// URIs,
checksums, byte sizes, and internal storage status values. Use `access.downloadUrl` when `availability`
is `available` and the server could produce a time-limited signed URL.

artifactId.description: Opaque artifact id for support correlation (not a storage path).

type.description: Coarse artifact kind for display and routing.

availability.description: Whether the artifact can be fetched (partner-safe lifecycle).

downloadUrl.description: HTTPS signed read URL when available; otherwise null.

expiresAt.description: When the signed URL expires, if applicable.

generatedAt.description: When the artifact record was created (approximate generation time).

PartnerWebhookResultReadyBody.description:
Outbound `result.ready` webhook JSON body (HTTPS POST). Minimal partner notification: correlate by ids and
load full detail via GET result APIs. Does not include internal tenant UUIDs, job ids, model output, or
correlation metadata (those remain in delivery records server-side). **Breaking change** from the prior
envelope that included `inference`, `tenantId`, and `processingJobId`.
Not an HTTP path on this API; documented here as the partner contract for callbacks.

reportId.description: Present when a report bundle artifact id is available on the result (prefers PDF, then JSON, then HTML).

PartnerResultDetailResponse.description:
Partner GET `/v1/results/:id`. Minimal fields: ids, submission status, completion time, public outcome
summary(ies), and artifact download handles. Multi-outcome runs include **`outcomes`** (array, same order
as stored inference bundle); **`outcome`** remains the primary (first) row for backward compatibility.
Omits internal job ids, subject linkage, model metadata, and citation placeholders.

completedAt.description: Wall time when the result row was last updated (completion / refresh).

outcome.description: The primary (first) outcome; `null` when not yet available.

outcomes.description:
Included when more than one wellbeing outcome was scored. Order matches the persisted multi-outcome
bundle. Omitted for single-outcome results (use `outcome` only).

notices.message: Some optional inputs were not provided.
